element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns 4-frame movement blocks and sword swing timing without level requirements imposed
Destroying monster lairs frees captured souls; souls reconstruct towns and NPCs provide advice/items
First RNG-dependent enemies; move in random directions, introducible off-screen behavior
Conveyor belt mechanics in boss arena; regional differences altered layout difficulty
NPCs grant items; level requirements gate certain weapon usage
Three sacred artifacts required to call Phoenix; used for final boss defeat
Flame attack patterns; regional versions changed safe-standing zones
One colored stone per region; required to open path to World of Evil
Sprite changed between regions; significant difficulty spike
Cosmetic regional variations; late-mid game difficulty
Story-critical; Dr. Leo imprisoned; gem drop rate increases (9/100 threshold)
King Magridd imprisoned; Floor Stalker enemies; gem drop rate 11/100
Highest gem drop rate (51/100 large); final villain Deathtoll; requires all six stones and Phoenix power

typical_relief_triggers: Defeating monster lair boss and liberating a soul
Town reconstruction with NPC dialogue and item rewards
Moving to new overworld area after region completion
Acquiring equipment upgrades from freed NPCs
typical_tension_triggers: Boss arena entry (conveyor belts, flame patterns, spatial hazards)
Random enemy actions requiring RNG prediction
Approaching World of Evil with gathered stones
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gradual escalation across six regional dungeons with soul-liberation rewards creating relief cycles; World of Evil dungeon culminates tension with final boss. Movement constraints (4-frame blocks, no diagonal movement) create deliberate pacing rhythm.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Grass Valley introduces movement mechanics and basic combat with plant enemies; minimal threat; Solid Arm/Metal Mantis first boss with moderate challenge via arena layout
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Regions 2-5 (GreenWood, St. Elles, Mountain of Souls, Dr. Leo's Laboratory) introduce varied bosses (Deathtoll with flame patterns, Medusa/Poseidon sprite variations, Mermaid Queen); gem drop rates increase (3-9/100 thresholds); puzzle complexity in dungeons escalates
difficulty_curve.late_game: Magridd Castle (11/100 gem rate) and World of Evil (51/100 gem rate) concentrate difficulty; requires all six magical stones and three sacred artifacts for Phoenix; Deathtoll final boss demands mastery of combat timing and RNG manipulation
notable_spikes: Deathtoll boss introduces complex flame attack patterns with regional difficulty variance
Medusa/Poseidon sprite change marks difficulty jump
World of Evil entry requires full preparation with stones and artifacts

notable_level_patterns.description: Monster lair → defeat boss → free soul → town reconstruction → NPC rewards → proceed to next region. Repeats across six regions with escalating enemy complexity and boss mechanics.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Grass Valley through Magridd Castle regions

notable_level_patterns.description: Boss arenas feature directional conveyor belts that alter fight approach. Solid Arm's arena conveyor direction reversed between Japanese and Western versions, changing tactical viability of luring boss to specific positions.

Plant enemies in early areas make random movement decisions triggering RNG calls. Off-screen enemy behavior affects RNG state. Frame timer at screen transitions creates calculable RNG sequences for TAS optimization.

Monster lairs drop gems (1/5/10 point values) at region-specific rates. Drop rates scale from lenient (1/4 thresholds in Grass Valley) to abundant (51/100 in World of Evil), incentivizing backtracking for resource farming.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All regions with scaling gem thresholds

notable_level_patterns.description: Each regional boss exhibits distinct attack patterns. Deathtoll uses flame attacks (safe zones altered regionally), Medusa/Poseidon sprite changed by region, Mermaid Queen cosmetic variations create learnable mechanical distinct encounters.
